capabilities: Implement constant-time comparison using cryptographic hashing to ensure security in EVM smart contracts.

procedure.action: Hash sensitive values using keccak256 before comparison to prevent timing attacks using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Invoke commit-reveal schemes to separate commitment and revelation phases, ensuring data integrity following the specified pattern; write a unit test covering the primary code path; assert test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete.

Compose zero-knowledge proofs to validate data without revealing the secret itself by composing module interfaces via adapter pattern; assert input/output type contracts match before connecting; assert integration_error_count < 1 after contract validation.

Test gas consumption patterns to ensure no observable differences during execution using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Release on a testnet and simulate attacks to verify resistance to timing analysis using blue-green deployment strategy; run smoke_test() against new instance before switching traffic; assert smoke_test_pass_rate >= 1.0 before promoting.

summary: Use keccak256 hashing for secret comparisons in Solidity to prevent timing attacks. Consider commit-reveal schemes or zero-knowledge proofs to avoid exposing sensitive data.
title: Constant-Time Comparison in Solidity Smart Contracts

failureConditions: Sensitive data exposed through timing analysis; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
Increased gas costs due to inefficient comparison methods.
Incorrect implementation leading to security vulnerabilities.
metrics: test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete
input/output type contracts match before connecting
successConditions: All sensitive comparisons use keccak256 hashing; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Commit-reveal scheme correctly separates phases; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Zero-knowledge proofs validate without revealing secrets; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
No significant gas cost variance in test scenarios; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
